compositional_truth_check.P2_Mexican_Mint: Systemic Loss (No viable mint foliage detected)
compositional_truth_check.P4_Silver_Guest: Present (Black Pot, shared with P2/P3 context)
compositional_truth_check.Anomalies: Presence of white perlite/debris in soil; Pothos leaf in P3 showing chlorosis/necrosis.

inventory_reconciliation.notes: The Mexican Mint (P2) is not visible as a living specimen. The Pothos (P3) is the primary occupant of the rabbit-anchored pot. P4 shows a single, isolated leaf structure.

plant_audit.P1: String of Nickels: Stable, low-light adapted, consistent foliage density.
plant_audit.P3: Pothos: Significant leaf degradation observed. The large leaf on the left is showing advanced chlorosis and necrosis (browning).
plant_audit.P4: Silver Guest: Single leaf structure remains, showing no significant growth or decline over the 5-day period.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistent moisture levels, no visible fungal blooms.
biome_observations.debris: White perlite scattered on surface; electronic sensor cables present in P3/P4.

temporal_deltas.T_minus_4_to_Current: Pothos (P3) leaf necrosis has progressed from the edges inward. The P4 leaf remains static. P1 shows no significant change.

visual_health_inference.P3: Declining (Nutrient deficiency or root stress suspected)

vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is currently under stress. The Pothos (P3) is exhibiting clear signs of physiological decline, specifically marginal necrosis on the primary leaf. The Mexican Mint (P2) is absent, suggesting a systemic loss. The environment is stable but the biological components are struggling to maintain vigor under the current lighting and soil conditions.
